title: OSIG going forward (the meritocracy reckoning, 2/10/23)
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir abandons the OSIG-president ambition after the Dinsdales party confirms via Ashley that the group runs on seniority, not meritocracy.
  • Three scenarios laid out: (1) stay full-effort and aim large-cap/DADCO manager, (2) take treasurer + minimum effort and pivot energy to Crypto Club / other projects, (3) leave OSIG entirely for Corvallis Crypto Club.
  • Picks scenario 2 as default — the half-in/half-out posture — contingent on having a real side-thing.
  • The Austin Consensus 2023 trip is teed up here as the next decision to iron out — first appearance of the Austin thesis.
  • Plans to consult Derek, Mom, and nonas — same family-counsel pattern that recurs across decision docs.

Notes

The doc is an early-2023 OSIG post-mortem on his presidency play. He cites his Coinbase pitch as proof-of-effort but acknowledges the system is subjective (Owen's observation: no measurable competency metric). Lauren is the only other person he thinks could match him in the role. The "all in / half in / all out" framing is an explicit decision matrix — same shape as the travel vs work matrix. Importantly: the Crypto Club is named as a long-shot dependent on outside investment — first written acknowledgment that the club needs capital. This is the proximate cause of the OEBVS outreach two months earlier and the Austin pivot that follows.
